behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection plus CLI classification at process boundary. MOCK-MISS: this is not just local counting logic; it depends on how rename entries are emitted from the repository scan. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: a mocked diff/status payload can miss real rename formats/threshold behavior and would hide subprocess/output parsing failures. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text (no AI/ML step; single CLI analysis flow).
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI via subprocess, and assert the stdout report has one rename and zero add/delete for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real git diff output for binary content and real process I/O/encoding behavior. REAL-INFRA: binary diff handling and decode/parse errors often appear only with real repo data and subprocess streams. MOCK-HIDE: fixtures with simplified text diffs can always pass while missing actual binary markers/byte edge cases. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow disqualified because no model-driven decision exists in the behavior.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a true binary file (e.g., image bytes), invoke the CLI as a subprocess, and assert exit success plus correct binary-file count in stdout report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at git command/exit-code boundary for unborn HEAD repositories. REAL-INFRA: no-commit repo behavior is produced by real git state and subprocess responses. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing git calls can mask actual stderr/return-code/path behavior and exception mapping. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by behavior text (pure CLI/git state handling, no AI sequence).
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real empty git repo with zero commits, run the CLI subprocess, and assert non-error exit plus an empty structured report on stdout.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Behavior validates end-to-end correctness against real repository history semantics, not only local arithmetic. MOCK-MISS: interaction between history extraction and category aggregation is the failure boundary. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: synthetic fixtures can miss real git history nuances (renames, deletions, mixed commits) and subprocess parsing issues. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow disqualified because behavior does not involve ML output quality or multi-agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known added/deleted/renamed/modified events across commits, run the CLI, and assert each reported count equals the ground-truth history constructed in the repo.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is git index vs working-tree state interpretation through the CLI’s real repository read path. REAL-INFRA: staged/unstaged semantics come from actual git index/worktree behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked status/diff snapshots can hide state-transition bugs and subprocess flag/path differences. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by behavior text (no AI/ML calls; single-step CLI comparison).
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create the same change once as unstaged and once staged, run the CLI in both states, and assert stdout structured reports are byte-for-byte identical.
